element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Customizable appearance and gender; player establishes control baseline
Foundational combat; skill increases through weapon usage over time
Party formation and AI-controlled ally tactics; up to 7 additional party members
Alternative combat approach; skill-based advancement through usage
Resource-gated through mana; skill advancement through usage
Found via enemy drops, container breaking, or vendor purchase
Invading force that prompts initial farm warning quest
Ancient threat resurgent after 300-year imprisonment; major campaign pivot
Player-configurable party behavior modifying character positioning and combat engagement
Spatial puzzle for resource management; mule character for expanded capacity
Economic system for equipment acquisition and trading
Resource-based healing and mana restoration; inventory-limited

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe town areas with merchants
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of higher-threat enemy races (Krug, Seck)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous action-driven rhythm with monster encounters and loot rewards interspersed; no loading screens maintain flow; party-based tactics allow tactical breathing room between full-intensity combat phases

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible baseline with farmer protagonist and melee combat introduction; party gradually assembles
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy variety increases with Krug encounters; ranged weapons and magic skills unlock for tactical variety
difficulty_curve.late_game: Seck revelation escalates stakes narratively; campaign culminates in addressing 300-year-old imprisoned threat
notable_spikes: Introduction of Krug invading force (narrative spike)
Revelation of Seck and their historical significance (thematic/difficulty escalation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeon Siege eschews traditional level breaks in favor of a single large world without loading screens. Player journeys through largely linear path encountering monsters in clusters while narrative quest markers guide progression.
Early encounters with solo/small party controlled via AI formations before full eight-character roster becomes available. Formation and aggression tactics serve as meaningful mechanical progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early campaign (first town and initial Krug encounters)

notable_level_patterns.description: Combat structured around waves of varying enemy types; player characters fight 'hordes of enemies' as core mechanic rather than individual duels. Party-based defense and coordinated tactics break monotony.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Goblin mines area (mentioned in documentation as typical combat scenario)

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns serve as safe spaces with vendors to purchase/sell equipment, providing resource management and tactical planning moments between combat-heavy wilderness sections.
